summary: Reconfigured the vendored tools/asm-differ setup for the dtk-era Melee build layout and refreshed asm-differ itself with broader architecture and relocation handling. The PR updated diff_settings.py from the old expected/build/wip paths and make flags to orig/GALE01, build/GALE01, main.elf.MAP, ninja, and dtk-style object directories. It also committed poetry.lock, added black/unit-test/poetry-lock GitHub workflows, added tests for SH2 parsing behavior, and expanded diff.py with SH2, SH4, SH4EL, and m68k support plus several relocation, binary-diff, and CLI improvements.
what_changed: Changed Melee asm-differ settings from legacy paths like expected/build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol, build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol, and build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map to dtk-style paths: orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol, build/GALE01/main.dol, and build/GALE01/main.elf.MAP.
Changed the configured build command from makeflags-oriented WIP settings to config["make_command"] = ["ninja"].
Changed build_dir to build/GALE01/src and expected_dir to build/GALE01/obj, matching the dtk object/output layout.
Committed tools/asm-differ/poetry.lock and removed poetry.lock from tools/asm-differ/.gitignore so dependency resolution is pinned and checkable.
Added GitHub Actions under tools/asm-differ/.github/workflows for black formatting, unit tests via python3 test.py, and poetry.lock consistency checking.
Updated black pre-commit configuration from psf/black 22.3.0 to psf/black-pre-commit-mirror 23.12.1.
Updated mypy configuration from Python 3.6 to Python 3.7 and included test.py in typed files.
Expanded asm-differ advertised architecture support in README and argparse text from MIPS/PPC/AArch64/ARM32 to also include SH2, SH4, and m68k.
Added objdump candidates for sh-elf-objdump, sh4-linux-gnu-objdump, and m68k-elf-objdump.
Renamed the user-facing object-file override option from --objfile/dest=objfile to --file/dest=file and generalized its help text from object files to files being diffed.
Changed stop-at-ret from a boolean flag to a counted option so -s stops at the first return, -ss at the second return, and so on.
Added --yes to automatically answer yes/no prompts, especially for using watch mode without make mode.
Renamed ms_map_address_offset to map_address_offset while retaining a fallback to the old setting name.
Moved map_address_offset application to binary start-address resolution after map lookup rather than folding it into the raw map-file search result.
Allowed binary diffing against config.file instead of only project.myimg, with validation that binary -f start addresses are integer expressions.
Removed fallback rewriting of expected object paths from /src/.c.o to /asm/.s.o; missing reference objects now fail directly at the computed expected_dir-relative path.
Added expression evaluation for integer arguments using a restricted AST/operator evaluator, replacing ast.literal_eval for values like address arithmetic expressions.
Improved relocation processing for MIPS R_MIPS_LITERAL, PPC branch relocations with .text+0x offsets, and i686/Watcom-style relocation forms such as WRTSEG, dir32, DISP32, OFF32, and OFFPC32.
Added AsmProcessorSH2, AsmProcessorM68k, SH2/SH4/SH4EL/M68K ArchSettings, SH2 branch/delay-slot sets, and generated m68k branch instruction sets.
Added unit tests focused on SH2 comment parsing, immediate normalization boundaries, and branch marker behavior.
smart_moves: The dtk configuration change updated all three critical anchors together: base DOL, built DOL, and map file. That avoids a half-migrated asm-differ setup where the tool builds one artifact but searches symbols or references in another layout.
Keeping map_format as mw while changing the map path to build/GALE01/main.elf.MAP preserves the expected parser behavior instead of treating the dtk map as a generic GNU map without evidence.
The map_address_offset rename is safer than a hard break because create_project_settings still falls back to ms_map_address_offset when the new key is absent.
Committing poetry.lock plus adding a lock-check workflow makes the vendored tool reproducible and prevents silent dependency drift.
Adding unit tests at the same time as parser/architecture changes gives future asm-differ edits concrete regression coverage for SH2 comment parsing, immediates, and branch detection.
The counted -s implementation captures a real decomp workflow issue: some functions have multiple return instructions, so a boolean stop-at-ret is too blunt.
The generic --file option is better aligned with the expanded binary diff path because the override can now refer to a binary file as well as an object file.
The restricted AST integer evaluator supports practical address expressions without using arbitrary eval.
decomp_lessons: When the project build system/layout changes, update asm-differ paths as a coherent set: base image, current image, map file, build_dir, expected_dir, and make command all need to agree.
For dtk/Ninja-era Melee work, asm-differ should target orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol and build/GALE01/main.dol with map data from build/GALE01/main.elf.MAP, according to this PR's configuration.
Prefer explicit expected object directories over ad hoc src-to-asm path rewriting; mismatches should fail at the path the tool actually computed.
Tooling migrations should include CI and dependency pinning, not only local config changes, because asm-differ behavior is sensitive to Python package versions.
Architecture support in asm-differ is more than an objdump flag: it needs immediate regexes, relocation regexes, branch instruction sets, delay-slot rules when applicable, and an AsmProcessor hook.
Parser tests should include exact objdump-looking text with tabs, labels, comments, immediates, and branch targets because small regex changes can alter diff output.
Keep backwards-compatible config-key fallbacks when renaming project settings used by downstream decomp projects.
naming_conventions: Use config["make_command"] = ["ninja"] for the dtk asm-differ build command rather than legacy makeflags in diff_settings.py.
Use map_address_offset as the generic setting name; ms_map_address_offset remains only as a compatibility fallback.
Use --file / Config.file for an override file being diffed; --objfile / objfile was removed in this PR.
Use architecture names sh2, sh4, sh4el, and m68k in ArchSettings/get_arch-compatible configuration.
For Melee dtk paths in diff_settings.py, this PR used orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol, build/GALE01/main.dol, build/GALE01/main.elf.MAP, build/GALE01/src, and build/GALE01/obj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For PPC GCC branch relocations, R_PPC_REL14/R_PPC_REL24 rows containing .text+0x can be handled by replacing the displayed branch target argument with the relocation's .text offset during pre_process, then skipping further relocation replacement for that case.
For MIPS R_MIPS_LITERAL, the relocation replacement strips the computed addend from the replacement symbol.
For i686/Watcom-style output, ignore WRTSEG relocation rows and tolerate relocation spellings such as dir32, DISP32, OFF32, and OFFPC32.
For i686 address immediates, the updated matcher handles calls, direct reloc uses, pointer-like operands, zero immediates, and offset forms, then preserves offset additions when needed.
For SH2, treat rts as the end-of-function instruction and include branch/delay-slot knowledge for bf, bf.s, bt, bt.s, bra, braf, bsr, bsrf, jmp, jsr, and rts.
For SH2 immediate normalization, tests assert conversion of signed-looking 8-bit immediates such as #-1 and #-128 into hex byte values like #0xff and #0x80.
For m68k, normalize objdump pointer syntax in pre_process and generate branch instruction names from condition-code combinations rather than hand-listing every spelling.
Use a counted stop_at_ret value to stop after the Nth architecture-specific return instruction instead of assuming the first return ends the desired diff range.
When binary diffing with --file, require the start address to be an integer expression because the map is not searched for a function name in that mode.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify tools/asm-differ/diff_settings.py after the dtk path migration.
Find later PRs that replace the TODO gc-wii-binutils in diff_settings.py.
Find PRs touching map_address_offset or removing ms_map_address_offset compatibility.
Find asm-differ PRs adding or changing SH2, SH4, SH4EL, or m68k support.
Find PRs that update tools/asm-differ from upstream or change the vendored poetry.lock.
